Barcelona Weekend Guide: A three-day Barcelona itinerary should include Gaudí's Sagrada Família (book tickets online weeks in advance), the surreal rooftop of Casa Batlló, and a stroll through the Gothic Quarter's medieval alleyways. Day two is perfect for Park Güell in the morning, the Picasso Museum in the afternoon, and an evening tapas crawl through El Born starting at Bar del Pla. The Barceloneta beach is walkable from the city center and best visited on weekday mornings before the crowds; the broad Passeig de Gràcia boulevard is ideal for architecture walks and shopping. Barcelona's metro system is efficient and inexpensive — a T-Casual 10-trip card covers most city-center movement.
